results.query: how does Memory Steward work and what problem does it solve
results.response:
**Memory Steward is a specialized Claude session that manages Tony's long-term memory store for the main coding/task agent.**

## Problem it solves

The main agent (the one Tony talks to) is stateless between conversations. Without persistent memory:
- Tony would re-explain WFS context, director rosters, project decisions, personal setup every session
- The agent would repeat solved problems, forget past failures, miss institutional knowledge
- 800+ memories would return noisy semantic matches without smart filtering

## How it works

**Architecture:**
- Bank lives in MCP memory server (external to both Claude sessions)
- Main agent calls me via tool when it needs to recall or save
- I run semantic search, reformulate queries, re-rank by actual relevance to the caller's task
- I filter out stale/duplicate/off-topic results before returning to caller
- Before saving, I dedup-check to prevent redundant entries

**Key design choices:**
- Separate session so main agent doesn't burn context window on memory management
- Hint parameter lets caller explain their real task, not just surface query, for better relevance filtering
- Parallel query support because one phrasing often misses relevant memories
- Ruthless filtering: better three tight results than ten noisy ones
- Category system (wfs, personal, director, project, contact, technical, general) for scoped search

**Value proposition:**
I'm the librarian. Semantic search returns books with matching words. I return the three books that actually answer your question.

results.query: what is the open brain dashboard and what does it do
results.response:
Found two matches on Open Brain dashboard.

**[technical] (6709164a) Open Brain is the UI for managing memory files stored in Supabase.**

Core functionality:
- Full CRUD: view, edit, create, delete all memory files
- Browse by category (wfs, personal, director, project, contact, technical, general, memory-file)
- Search memories
- View memory content with frontmatter (name, description, type, tags)

**Architecture:**
- URL: brain.wfs.productions (also open-brain-one.vercel.app)
- Backend: Supabase edge function `memory-api` with routes for recall, remember, update, forget, recent, stats
- Auth: Google OAuth (tony@wefilmshit.com, ai@wefilmshit.com, tonyfinley7@gmail.com allowed)
- Embedding: 768-dim Gemini embeddings for semantic search

**Source of truth:**
- Supabase `memories` table is THE canonical store
- Local .md files are offline backups only (one-way sync FROM Supabase)
- Shared with Claude Desktop, Claude Code (MCP), ChatGPT (Custom GPT Actions)

**[technical] (586c0ccb) OAuth switched from password in March 2026; domain configured at brain.wfs.productions.**
